title: Motion In a Plane Test - 3 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: A man walks along an inclined plane at a uniform speed of $5\,\text{ms}^{-1}$. The plane is inclined at $37^\circ$ to the horizontal. While moving down the plane, he observes that the rain appears to fall straight vertically downward. When he moves up the same plane with the same speed, he observes that the rain appears to make an angle $\theta = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{7}{8}\right)$ with the horizontal. The speed of the rain is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Velocity of the river with respect to ground is given by $v_0$. Width of the river is $d$. A swimmer swims (with respect to water) perpendicular to the current with acceleration $a = 2t$ (where $t$ is time) starting from rest from the origin $O$ at $t=0$. The equation of trajectory of the path followed by the swimmer is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A flag on a bus is fluttering in north direction & wind is blowing in east direction. Then which of the following will be true -
options: bus is moving in north east direction.
bus may be moving in any direction between south & east.
bus may be moving in any direction between south & west.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A man who is wearing a hat of extended length of 12 cm is running in rain falling vertically downwards with speed 10 m/s. The maximum speed with which man can run, so that rain drops do not fall on his face (the length of his face below the extended part of the hat is 16 cm) will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: It is raining vertically downwards with a velocity of $3 \text{ km h}^{-1}$. A man walks in the rain with a velocity of $4 \text{ km h}^{-1}$. The rain drops will fall on the man with a relative velocity of :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A boat, which has a speed of 5 km/h in still water, crosses a river of width 1 km along the shortest possible path in 15 minutes. The velocity of the river water in km/h is -
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A man crosses the river perpendicular to river flow in time t seconds and travels an equal distance down the stream in T seconds. The ratio of man's speed in still water to the speed of river water will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The river velocity is $5 \mathrm{ms^{-1}}$. A swimmer starts from point A and reaches point B after 2 s. The distance AB is $(8 + 6\sqrt{3})$ m. Find the velocity of swimmer with respect to river.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A boat is rowed across a river (perpendicular to river flow) at the rate of 9 km/hr. The river flows at the rate of 12 km/hr. The velocity of boat in km/hr is:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A man walks in rain with a velocity of $5\text{kmh}^{-1}$ The rain drops strike at him at an angle of $45^\circ$ with the horizontal. The velocity of the rain if it is falling vertically downward is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The velocity of swimmer $A$ and $B$ in still water is $5 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. Swimmer $A$ decides to cross the river in minimum time and swimmer $B$ decides to cross the river through shortest path. They start from the same point simultaneously and at $t=t_0$, the distance of swimmer $A$ from the bank is 25 m and that of $B$ is 20 $\mathbf{m}$. Find speed of stream of river (in $\mathbf{m s}^{\mathbf{- 1}}$ ). (Assume the velocity of steam is lesser than the velocity of swimmer in water). Bank is from where they started.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A boat which can move with a speed of 5 m/s relative to water crosses a river of width 480 m flowing with a constant speed of 4 m/s. What is the time taken by the boat to cross the river along the shortest path?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A pipe is mounted on a cart. It can be rotated in the vertical plane. The cart moves uniformly along a horizontal path with a velocity of $2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. If at angle $$\theta $$ to the horizontal should the pipe be placed so that the rain drops falling vertically with a velocity of $6 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ enter the pipe moving parallel to its wall. Then find the value of tan $$\theta $$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A swimmer crosses the river along the line making an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with the direction of flow. Velocity of the river water is $5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Swimmer takes 12 seconds to cross the river of width 60 m . The velocity of the swimmer with respect to water will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: While driving his scooter, a boy noticed that the raindrops strike him straight down with a speed of $10 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$. If the scooter is moving along a level (horizontal) road with a speed of $10 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$, determine the direction in which the rain is actually falling.
questions.subject: Physics
